title: Buchenavia guianensis
intro: Buchenavia guianensis, also known as "Cedrelina," is a medicinal plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its potential health benefits such as antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Buchenavia guianensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into herbal remedies, such as capsules and ointments.
taxonomy: Buchenavia guianensis belongs to the family Combretaceae. It is a genus of flowering plants native to the Neotropics. The Combretaceae family is an extensive group of flowering plants that are mostly found in tropical and subtropical regions. This family includes many species of trees and shrubs, often known for their distinctive flowers and fruits. Buchenavia guianensis is one of the many species within this family.
